titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 577

p.seg: Malcolm, king of the Scots, to the prior of Coldingham and the whole community of that same place, and to all worthy men of his entire land, greeting.

Know me to have allowed and resolutely instructed that the prior and monks of Coldingham may employ their own men according to their will, wherever they abide within their land, for the building of the toun of Coldingham, and I forbid on pain of my full forfeiture that anyone disturb them in respect of the said business.
As witnesses: Walter the chancellor, Herbert the chamberlain, Richard the chaplain, Nicholas the clerk.
